label: About
title: About The Vermilion Arc Astrolabe
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 16 recent saved-signal files and 12 captured links, then narrowed the page to 8 source windows around Into the Light, Flower Music, Shu Qin, Surf Noir, Brise, and three tweet-backed media signals. A Bandcamp image from Marisa Anderson helped set the red field, pale arcs, and sun-worn material direction. The scene became The Vermilion Arc Astrolabe: a minimal vermilion field with a half-dome astrolabe, a low smoked horizon, and small lens-like marks that hold the sources. After the image was made, the mask pass used geometry and contour candidates to attach source windows to the visible marks.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
